reviews.review_msg: It was a nice class, I really enjoyed it. Make sure to participate and attend all lectures.

His tests are somewhat challenging, and he's a tough grader, too. Although he's pleasant and reasonable. However, he appears to be preoccupied with many duties (other than teaching) and can be very unorganized at times. He's also got this liberal internationalist agenda going on, make of that what you will.

My 4th time taking classes with Rado, which is the best experience I had in undergrad. He is a prof that genuinely cares about students' futures and always willing to provide guidance. Lectures are connected with his experience in diplomacy, encouraging students put insight into a real-world scenario. Everyone can ace the class if you participate.

Robert has a lot of experience in the field he teaches, not only academically but professionally. His lectures are very straight-forward and also very thought provoking. He is considerate and eager to help. Do your work and you will see the rewards.

Ross is a very fair man. You have to do all the reading to excel in his class. Is also a very writing intensive course, just keep in mind that this is a 4th year course and 4th year level course work is expected.
Fun and engaging classes with a passionate prof. Lectures are long but worth it - use unstuck study alongside the textbook to ace this class.

One of the best professors at SFU for polisci. I've had James 2x before and im in the middle of my 3rd course w/ him. Is super chill (even in a 400-level course), also kind and approachable. He uses the exact same grade weight in all of his classes so you know what to expect. Lectures are long but u can tell he's passionate about what he teaches!!

I dropped this course after the professor sent a passive-aggressive email before I even attended, unwelcoming me for enrolling late. He told me to drop without asking if I had prior international relations coursework, which I do. No reason to be rude on a first interaction. Best to avoid.
